channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-17566] Refactor ucred handler code in mdt
item.description:
user credentials in MDT code, managed in struct ucred, has 2 flavors: new and old. On top of that, the way the appropriate data is given to those functions differs depending on reint or not reint code. This makes the whole user credential management very hard to follow. There are lots of possibility to clean that, refactoring the code under a unique place. Let's try to do that.

  • old flavor (old_init_ucred, old_init_ucred_reint, old_init_ucred_common)

Most of Lustre RPCs use this in the absence of GSS mode being setup

  • new flavor (new_init_ucred)

Mainly use when GSS is on. This leverage a new struct ptlrpc_user_desc sent by client only when GSS is on.

This flavor also introduce a 'perm' feature which is not really documented. A /etc/lustre/perm.conf config file exists for that.

Fun facts, Kit had the same questioning already 7 years ago:

 

commit 37db778f48f952747575e323cb341ed663852fff
Author: Kit Westneat <kit.westneat@gmail.com>
Date:   Fri Apr 14 11:06:02 2017 -0400
    LU-9145 nodemap: new_init_ucred doesn't do nodemapping
    
    The new_init_ucred path was missed in the original nodemap
    implementation. This patch adds the mapping calls to new_init_ucred.
    
    WIP: There are some issues/questions:
     - some of the new_init_ucred code should be merged with
            old_init_ucred_common.
     - Why does new_init check for setuid/setgid/setgrp, but not old_init?
     - What is ptlrpc_user_desc and should it interact with nodemap?
item.summary: Refactor ucred handler code in mdt

status.description: This issue was once resolved, but the resolution was deemed incorrect. From here issues are either marked assigned or resolved.

comments.comment:
"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/55025/
Subject: LU-17566 mdt: improve new_init_ucred() for refactoring
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: 2752ac20422b681649e7a1c9ab0b6cf0f93d9e27

"Aurelien Degremont <adegremont@nvidia.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/55025
Subject: LU-17566 mdt: improve new_init_ucred() for refactoring
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 98c356ba0a4ff84d098d750aa5b27fa1ca8a0607

"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/54194/
Subject: LU-17566 mdt: move squash code in new/old_init_ucred
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: 1730d8093fb36e7957414d314755ae5208da1011

"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/54111/
Subject: LU-17566 mdt: remove duplicate call to mdt_init_ucred_reint()
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: 65e0802f2ada98f802d01b5672bb9349ad0dde8c

Hi Aurelien,

Regarding the test for CAP_DAC_READ_SEARCH, my guess is that it was added to address a specific use case, presumably related to NFS export given the references in the commit comment of https://review.whamcloud.com/17815. And I bet this was not tried with a GSS flavor in place, which explains why they did not think about new_init_ucred().
My advice would be to also add the cap_raised test in new_init_ucred(), so that it gets aligned with old_init_ucred_common().

Regarding patch https://review.whamcloud.com/33316 , indeed uc_ginfo is only used by the new flavor. But I agree with you, that would make sense to also reset supp groups (uc->uc_suppgids[0] = uc->uc_suppgids[1] = -1) with the old flavor in case the group is squashed.

Thanks,
Sebastien.

Sebastien, also, you wrote https://review.whamcloud.com/33316 . That's one diff between new and old code. If GID is squashed, we skip filling uc_ginfo, but we also reset suppgids values. ginfo is always empty in old_init_ucred() code path, but should we reset suppgids for old_init_ucred()? That would make sense to me...

Thanks Sebastien for the clarification about uc_cap, that's helpful!

I have another question.

 

                        if (unlikely(PTR_ERR(identity) == -EREMCHG ||
                                     cap_raised(uc->uc_cap,
                                                CAP_DAC_READ_SEARCH))) {
                                identity = NULL;

old_init_ucred_common() has this check, with the specific cap_raised() test. For the same code, new_init_ucred() does not have it.

 

This test was introduced by https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/17815, which missed new_init_ucred(). This patch is itself of fixup for another commit, which, this one, modified both new_init_ucred() and old_init_ucred(). Since 17815 was landed, the code was changed a lot, and nothing remains of it, except for this line.

I'm wondering if we should remove it, or have it in both functions?

"Aurelien Degremont <adegremont@nvidia.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/54194
Subject: LU-17566 mdt: move squash code in new/old_init_ucred
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 37945eecf986a1ffeb3366a14e5a90092a68ddd0

where the old_init_ucred() does not. the cap from the RPC is copied unconditionally of the squash config.
Who is correct here?

Actually they both are

This is just that the code path with the 'old' fashion is a little bit different. In old_init_ucred(), the capabilities are initially set:

uc->uc_cap = CAP_EMPTY_SET;
	ll_set_capability_u32(&uc->uc_cap, body->mbo_capability);

	rc = old_init_ucred_common(info, nodemap);

But then in old_init_ucred_common(), there is:

if (nodemap && uc->uc_o_uid == nodemap->nm_squash_uid) {
		[...]
		uc->uc_cap = CAP_EMPTY_SET;

So in the end this is the same: capabilities are set only if the user is not squashed.

Looking into the code, it looks like new_init_ucred() is never called for Krb5, it seems to be reserved to SSK because of this function:

   static inline int flvr_allows_user_desc(__u32 flavor)
   {
   	return (SPTLRPC_FLVR_POLICY(flavor) == SPTLRPC_POLICY_GSS &&
   		(SPTLRPC_FLVR_MECH(flavor) == SPTLRPC_MECH_GSS_NULL ||
   		 SPTLRPC_FLVR_MECH(flavor) == SPTLRPC_MECH_GSS_SK));
   }

Because of this function, the PTLRPC_SEC_FL_UDESC flag is always cleared on requests when a Kerberos flavor is in place (see flavor_set_flags()). So ultimately when we are about to choose between old_init_ucred() and new_init_ucred() (from __mdt_init_ucred() or mdt_init_ucred_reint()), we always pick the 'old' implementation if Kerberos is enforced:

   	if (!req->rq_auth_gss || req->rq_auth_usr_mdt || !req->rq_user_desc)
   		return old_init_ucred(info, body);
   	else
   		return new_init_ucred(info, BODY_INIT, body);

Looking at Git history, this has been like that from 2015 at least, when SSK was implemented.

So good news is that the 'new' ucred init implementation is used for SSK, so this is functional code. But we would need to take much care if we were to replace for Kerberos the 'old' ucred init implementation with the new one, as there might be some corner cases. I am also curious about the reason why it was decided to create the 'new' implementation, instead of just improving the 'old' existing one. Note that 'old' and 'new' might have been coexisting for a while, I can see a commit with both methods that dates back from 2007...

Sebastien, looking at the refactoring, I notice one diff I'd like your take on it:

new_init_ucred() has this snippet of code:

if (!nodemap || ucred->uc_o_uid != nodemap->nm_squash_uid)
           ll_set_capability_u32(&ucred->uc_cap, pud->pud_cap);

where the old_init_ucred() does not. the cap from the RPC is copied unconditionally of the squash config.

Who is correct here?
Peter, I saw you assigned that ticket to me. I'll try to send some patches to help, but I'm not sure I'll take the whole ownership of it

I think it would be better, as you might be suggesting, to enhance the old fashion with bits taken from the new one.

This does not have to be done in one step. We could do that incrementally. My idea would be to adapt the code to make the 2 functions looking the same, before the merge. Actually, this is not that hard.

Also, are we sure the new fashion is used as soon as a GSS flavor is enforced, or is it only for GSS specific requests?

There are very few GSS specific RPCs, right? And they don't deal with user perm. From what I understood from the code at the moment, the go thru the new code, you need GSS on and to be on a OSS or MGS.

I would be quite reluctant to get rid of the old fashion. As opposed to the new fashion, the old fashion is largely used, so at least we know it works. I think it would be better, as you might be suggesting, to enhance the old fashion with bits taken from the new one. Also, are we sure the new fashion is used as soon as a GSS flavor is enforced, or is it only for GSS specific requests?

"Aurelien Degremont <adegremont@nvidia.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/54111
Subject: LU-17566 mdt: remove duplicate call to mdt_init_ucred_reint()
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 8035d1e3c50d4861c377601401e969cf440c29d9
